prose:
Furan Fukui reactivity descriptors were computed using DFT at the B3LYP/def2-TZVP level (tier 'standard', density-fitted) in the gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1, in PySCF. The input geometry was a user-supplied .xyz file used as a single-point calculation without re-optimization. Fukui functions (f⁺, f⁻, f⁰) were computed from Mulliken charges of the neutral, cationic (N+1, doublet), and anionic (N−1, doublet) states.

Key findings: The two equivalent sp² carbons at ring positions 4 and 5 are the most reactive sites toward electrophilic attack (f⁺ ≈ 1.92, f⁰ ≈ 1.04 each). The oxygen atom shows large negative dual descriptor (−0.62), indicating reduced electrophilic reactivity. Charge conservation was clean (Σf⁺ = 1.0000, Σf⁻ = 1.0000), confirming convergence of all ionic-state calculations. The calculation produced a visualization plot and complete per-atom Fukui and dual-descriptor values.

No warnings were reported. Integrity status: trustworthy.</prose>
